title: Llon Banjeer

{{Quote|And I would remind the Council that each and every one of us has profited from the sudden vacuum left by Jax's and Wessel's untimely deaths… just as we did from Palpatine's.|Burr Nolyds|<ref name="CEII 2" />}}
'''Llon Banjeer''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in command of [[Banjeer's fleet|one]] of the Empire's largest [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. In the aftermath of the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] debacle in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Banjeer allied himself with [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] before leading an [[Battle of the Hast Shipyards|attack]] on the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Hast Shipyards]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, the admiral was one of the [[Crimson Empire|backers]] of former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]] and aspiring [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Carnor Jax]]'s plan to sabotage [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Cloning/Legends|clones]]. Banjeer, as a representative of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], served as a member of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends#The "Council of Blood"|Imperial Interim Ruling Council]] under both Jax and the wealthy but arrogant [[Burr Nolyds]]. The admiral used his power to establish [[Boss Banjeer|his nephew]] as leader of [[Baramorra]] and all of the [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. A [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentrist]] and Imperial traditionalist, Admiral Banjeer disliked serving alongside the various [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]] present on the Council.

Following the assassination of Nolyds by [[Yuuzhan Vong]] agent [[Nom Anor]], Banjeer was the first to suggest that the culprit was Royal Guard [[Kir Kanos]]. He also suggested that the head of the Council would always be a target, and it was for that reason that the Council elected the wealthy bureaucrat [[Xandel Carivus]] to succeed Nolyds, hoping that the assassin would strike again and kill the least-liked member of the ruling council. Banjeer supported Carivus's nomination, commenting that at least the nominee was Human. The admiral would ultimately become Nom Anor's second victim, though as with Nolyds's murder, the assassination was made to look like the handiwork of Kir Kanos.

==Biography==
{{Quote|We all knew that a killer had targeted the council, yet you took ''no'' special precautions to ''protect'' us. Now Admiral Banjeer has been murdered because of your ''lax'' attitude!|Feena D'Asta to Xandel Carivus|<ref name="CEII 2" />}}
Llon Banjeer<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] serving as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], commanding [[Banjeer's fleet|one]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy's]] largest [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] first [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="Handbook 2" /> In the aftermath of the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] death, Banjeer became an ally of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Zsinj]], [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the [[Quelii Oversector]].<ref name="EGW" /> In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Atlas" /> when Imperial agents discovered the location of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Hast Shipyards]], the admiral prepared a fleet to attack the facility. After obtaining several [[Bulk cruiser/Legends|bulk cruisers]] from the wealthy [[D'Asta family]] [[D'Asta private fleet|fleet]] and converting them into [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighter]] [[Carrier/Legends|carriers]], Banjeer [[Battle of the Hast Shipyards|assaulted]] the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], destroying or crippling over thirty New Republic vessels—many of which were to be used against Zsinj's forces.<ref name="EGW" />

Around [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Banjeer was one of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] officers to become a member of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends#The "Council of Blood"|Imperial Interim Ruling Council]], an Imperial governing body established following the defeat at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].<ref name="CEII 1" /> While on the Council, Banjeer represented the interests of the Imperial Navy.<ref name="Handbook 2" /> Using the authority of his new position, Banjeer established [[Boss Banjeer|his nephew]] as the leader of the temperate world [[Baramorra]], and, in turn, the new leader of Baramorra used his uncle's connections to gain control of all the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], allowing him to keep the population in line.<ref name="Kenix Kil">''[[The Bounty Hunters: Kenix Kil]]''</ref> Admiral Banjeer, among others on the Council, conspired with the aspiring [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsman]] [[Carnor Jax]] to have Palpatine's [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] sabotaged by his [[Emperor's physician|physician]], thus paving the way for Jax's [[Crimson Empire|ascension to Emperor]].<ref name="CE">''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]''</ref> After the death of Carnor Jax on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] at the hands of fellow Royal Guard [[Kir Kanos]], the arrogant aristocratic [[Burr Nolyds]] became head of the Council, and Banjeer continued to serve under him.<ref name="CEII 1" />

At a meeting of the Interim Ruling Council in [[De-Purteen]] on [[Ord Cantrell/Legends|Ord Cantrell]], during which the state of the Empire was discussed, Council member [[Feena D'Asta]] suggested that the Council sue for peace with the New Republic. Nolyds announced that such a deal would not be made; Jax's plan had paved the way for the Council's control of the remnants of the Empire, and any attempt at negotiations would spell destruction for them. As Nolyds departed, he proclaimed that the deaths of Jax and [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Redd Wessel]], much like Palpatine's deaths, had benefited all of the Council members. Shortly thereafter, Nolyds was assassinated when a [[message disk]] planted by [[Yuuzhan Vong]] agent [[Nom Anor]],<ref name="CEII 1" /> stirring upon chaos in preparation for a galactic [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] by his people,<ref name="Vector Prime">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref> self-destructed in his [[Burr Nolyds's chambers|private chambers]].<ref name="CEII 1" />

[[File:Banjeer-CE2-02-08.jpg|thumb|left|The late Admiral Banjeer]]
The Council members suspected that the assassin had been one of their own, but Banjeer put forth the idea that Kir Kanos was the culprit, and that the head of the council would always be a target. It was for that reason that at a subsequent meeting of the council, [[Xandel Carivus]], a wealthy bureaucrat and native of Ord Cantrell, was nominated by the [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Za]]—on the suggestion of the [[Myke]] former [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Norym Kim]]—to succeed Nolyds as the leader of the Interim Ruling Council. Banjeer supported Carivus's nomination because, in accordance with his own [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentrist]] views, Carivus was a Human. The Council members did not realize that their new leader was a puppet of Nom Anor, and thus would not be assassinated as Nolyds had been.<ref name="CEII 1" />

The same could not be said for Banjeer, however. While waiting for his dinner, the admiral was fatally stabbed by Nom Anor, who used a knife bearing the insignia of Kir Kanos. One of Banjeer's [[Aide/Legends|aides]] discovered the body while delivering the admiral's meal. To prevent any further losses, the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] guards were doubled and all personnel were put on full alert. At a following Council meeting, the councilor [[General/Legends|General]] [[Immodet]] accused the [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] members of the Council of being behind the assassinations of Nolyds and Banjeer. Though the members in question denied any involvement, Admiral [[Jeratai]], who also had a seat on the Council and a fleet of his own, concurred with Immodet's assessment of the situation. Feena D'Asta accused Carivus of not taking precautions to prevent a further execution following Nolyds's death, and that his lax attitude had been responsible for Banjeer's death. Using the evidence gathered from both crime scenes, head of security [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Ganner]] incorrectly concluded that Kir Kanos was behind the murders, having discovered that the insignia on the [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] used to kill Nolyds and the symbol on the knife used to kill Banjeer matched Kanos's own Royal Guard crest. Carivus responded by ordering that Jeratai's and Banjeer's fleets be sent out to find and capture Kanos.<ref name="CEII 2" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|After all, Carivus ''is'' Human…|Banjeer|<ref name="CEII 2" />}}
An Imperial traditionalist<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref><ref name="Handbook 2" /> and a mild Humanocentrist,<ref name="CEII 1" /> Banjeer disliked the presence of non-Humans on the Interim Ruling Council<ref name="CSWE" /><ref name="Handbook 2" /> and was opposed to the thought of a non-Human becoming the leader of the Council, preferring someone like Xandel Carivus—whom the Council members despised—over a non-Human. Unlike his comrades, Banjeer was not so quick to accuse a member of the Interim Council of being behind Nolyds's assassination, believing that Kir Kanos was continuing his vendetta against all those who had sabotaged Palpatine's clones.<ref name="CEII 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Admiral Banjeer, albeit as an unnamed member of the Ruling Council, first appeared in [[Crimson Empire 1|issue one]] of [[1997]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]'', written by [[Mike Richardson]] and [[Randy Stradley]] and penciled by [[Paul Gulacy]].<ref name="CE1">[[Crimson Empire 1|''Crimson Empire'' 1]]</ref> Banjeer would go on to appear (and be given a name) in [[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 1|issues one]]<ref name="CEII 1" /> and [[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 2|two]] of [[1998]]'s [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'']] before being killed off.<ref name="CEII 2" /> He would be mentioned in ''[[The Bounty Hunters: Kenix Kil]]''; in it, his nephew was introduced.<ref name="Kenix Kil" /> The admiral was also given brief entries in ''[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire]]''<ref name="Handbook 2" /> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" /> ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' makes reference to a Banjeer family estate in [[Sirpar Hills]] on [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]], though that particular estate has not been tied to this Banjeer.<ref name="CCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> In [[2012]], he was given the first name "Llon" in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''.<ref name="EGW" />
